title: Light, Rich Korean Lard Dumplings
description: A crowd-pleasing Korean dish featuring light, crispy dumplings wrapped in a rich lard-based dough. This dish is perfect for a raw-friendly dinner option.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together flour and salt.
Add in pork lard and water, mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into small balls, about the size of a golf ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and green onions in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape, pressing the edges together to seal.
In a large pot or steamer, combine water and soy sauce.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer.
Add the dumplings to the pot, cover with a lid.
Steam the dumplings for 8-10 minutes, or until cooked through.
